signal_title: Public Consultation on the DSA Transparency Database
risk_rationale: The regulatory guidance from the EU requires platforms to submit statements explaining reasons for content removal, which aligns with transparency obligations but introduces a new requirement for public submission to a centralized database. This represents a moderate risk as it requires process adjustments but does not pose an existential threat.

compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Platforms must submit statements explaining reasons for content removal to a public database.
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The current baseline requires biannual transparency reports, but the new regulation requires real-time or frequent submissions to a public database.

remediations.title: Implement Real-Time Content Removal Statement Submission
remediations.description: Develop a system to generate and submit statements explaining content removal decisions to the EU DSA Transparency Database in real-time or as required.
acceptance_criteria: System generates content removal statements with required details.
Statements are submitted to the EU DSA Transparency Database as per regulatory timelines.

business_impact: The requirement to submit content removal statements to a public database may increase operational overhead and require additional resources for compliance. However, it enhances transparency and aligns with EU regulatory expectations, potentially improving trust with EU users.
recommended_actions: Assess current content moderation decision documentation processes.
Develop automated tools for generating and submitting required statements.
Engage with EU regulatory bodies to clarify submission timelines and formats.
